title: gfx950 versus gfx1200 ROCm Portability Path

problem.summary: Document the ROCm portability split between CDNA4-class gfx950 and RDNA4-class gfx1200 targets, including compile flags, feature probes, library baselines, and custom-kernel fallback dispatch.
inputs: GPU model and reported gfx target
hipcc target list and compile flags
candidate kernels with portable and target-specific paths
outputs: compile and dispatch matrix for gfx950 and gfx1200
constraints: Template-only; no gfx950 or gfx1200 timing, ISA, or profiler-counter evidence is claimed
Verify exact architecture flags with the installed ROCm toolkit before using target-specific paths
Keep data-center and workstation/deployment evidence separate

verification.method: Compile the portable baseline and each target-specific candidate with explicit offload targets, then attach task-specific correctness checks before timing claims.

recommended_shapes: memory tile copy 256MiB aligned and strided

curation.notes: Second parity pass analog for CUDA task `sm100-sm120-blackwell-path`, rewritten for ROCm GFX targets and evidence rules.
